g.text: 64-Round State Evolution — 'abc' Test Vector (NIST FIPS 180-4 Appendix B.1)
Final Digest: SHA-256('abc') — NIST FIPS 180-4 Appendix B.1
bytes 24..31 | b410ff61 f20015ad | H[6] H[7] big-endian
Bug Diagnosis: If your values diverge from NIST B.1
wrong a at t=0 | check SIGMA_UPPER_0 constants (2,13,22) vs sigma_lower (7,18,3)
wrong e at t=0 | check SIGMA_UPPER_1 constants (6,11,25) and CH formula
diverges t>0 | check variable rotation order: read all old values before assign
wrong T1 | verify K[t] table — K[0]=0x428a2f98 (cbrt(2) frac x 2^32)
wrong T2 | verify MAJ formula: (a&b)^(a&c)^(b&c) not (a&b)|(a&c)|(b&c)
H update wrong | must use += not = ; any = discards Merkle-Damgard feed-forward
masking missing | Python/JS: apply & 0xFFFFFFFF after every + ; C uint32 wraps OK
NIST | B.1 round 0 — verify a=0x5d6aebb1 e=0xf70e5939
NIST | B.1 round 1 — verify a=0x568f3f2f e=0xd04e5fc0
NIST | B.1 round 2 — verify a=0x34ffc9c1 e=0xdc1eab4c
Each round: same formula T1+T2, register rotation
K[t] from cube-root-of-prime table (all 64 values fixed)
W[t] from message schedule expansion (sigma recurrence)
Validate all rounds vs NIST PDF: sha256_debug_rounds()
Any divergence → check sigma constants, CH/MAJ formula, K table
a | (compute & compare) | final working var a
b | (compute & compare) | final working var b
c | (compute & compare) | final working var c
d | (compute & compare) | final working var d
e | (compute & compare) | final working var e
f | (compute & compare) | final working var f
g | (compute & compare) | final working var g
h | (compute & compare) | final working var h
NIST | B.1 post-round 63 table — must match exactly
+= is mod 2^32 — uint32_t wraps automatically in C
T1 = h + SIGMA_UPPER_1(e) + CH(e,f,g) + K[t] + W[t]
